title: Alsine decandra
intro: Alsine decandra, also known as Alsine, is a perennial plant species within the Saxifragaceae family, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the treatment of rheumatism and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Alsine decandra include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Alsine decandra include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and bronchitis.
uses: Alsine decandra, also known as Four-leafed Crowberry, is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
